description: Report-only triage for registered tests that are not yet owned by tools/test-quality/module-coverage.json. The expected_count values make unmapped-test drift visible without forcing ambiguous tests into the wrong product module.

entries.reason: Mixed FAUST UI metadata, generated DSP, and DslProcessor bridge coverage. Mapping it directly to audio/DSP would hide the DSL/tooling contract that needs explicit ownership.
entries.next_action: Decide whether DSL/native-language processor support becomes its own module or is split across audio/DSP, CLI/tooling, and view/import by test-name patterns.

entries.reason: CTest entries use the generic cmake command for mixed CMake, AU validator, install-layout, SDK guard, and Three.js capture checks. Command basename alone is not enough to infer product ownership.
entries.next_action: Add script/test-name aware mapping or explicit module labels for each cmake-backed test before assigning them to modules.

entries.reason: Python wrapper tests cover token checks, refactor guards, MCP binary smoke, Figma export, import fidelity, and gallery diff helpers. The interpreter basename hides the real contract.
entries.next_action: Teach the report to inspect script paths or add explicit capability/module labels for Python-backed CTest entries.

entries.reason: Shell-backed tests span install, signing, MCP launcher, Spectr capture, workspace freshness, retry logic, GPU requirement checks, and auval coverage. The shell command is not a useful module signal.
entries.next_action: Map shell-backed tests by script path/test name or replace generic shell commands with labelled CTest wrappers.

entries.reason: Matrix tests exercise generated example/plugin combinations and smoke-style product assembly, not one core runtime module.
entries.next_action: Decide whether example/plugin matrix coverage belongs in a product-assembly module, smoke tier, or format-adapter module.

entries.reason: Spectr regressions appear view/WebCompat-adjacent, but they need explicit import/runtime guarantee wording before being absorbed by the broad view/UI module.
entries.next_action: Review Spectr regression names and map each to a view/WebCompat guarantee or a separate import-regression capability.

entries.reason: These are NOT_BUILT placeholder CTest entries with no usable command. They should be handled as build/registration placeholders, not normal module coverage.
entries.next_action: Decide whether NOT_BUILT entries should stay registered, be labelled as disabled placeholders, or be excluded from module coverage accounting.

entries.reason: The headless binary currently covers unrelated contracts: format-registry factory replacement and AutoUI fallback behavior. A command-level module assignment would be misleading.
entries.next_action: Split by test-name pattern or move one contract to a more specific binary before mapping.

entries.reason: Waveform recorder tests bridge UI-visible recording behavior and audio/runtime data capture. The product guarantee needs explicit wording before module assignment.
entries.next_action: Define whether waveform recording is owned by view/UI, audio observability, or runtime capture, then map it accordingly.

entries.reason: The Node wrapper hides the actual Three.js/JSC bundle smoke test contract from command-pattern matching.
entries.next_action: Map Node-backed CTest entries by script path or apply an explicit WebCompat/tooling module label.

entries.reason: This demo executable is a smoke/platform-view signal rather than a focused unit-test module contract.
entries.next_action: Classify demo executable coverage as smoke/platform/view or replace it with narrower tests that state the product guarantee.
